reasoning: Dan Osborn actively established an exploratory committee for the 2026 US Senate race in Nebraska against incumbent Sen. Pete Ricketts on April 3, 2025. Following this, he has been aggressively releasing polling (tying him with Ricketts) and fundraising, positioning himself publicly for a race. Under FEC regulations, exploratory efforts that involve fundraising and polling often quickly trigger the $5,000 threshold that mandates formal registration and a candidacy announcement within a short window. A mid-April report noted a "FEC warning" regarding Osborn's activities, suggesting that his operational scale is forcing official candidate designation very soon. His rapid series of campaign-like actions (rallies, internal polling, broad fundraising appeals against a specific opponent) points to an imminent official announcement. The date June 1, 2025 is just under 6 weeks away. The combination of his established momentum and legal/FEC pressures to formally declare his candidacy points to a high likelihood of an announcement by June 1, 2025. Because some candidates artificially delay "official" announcements, my probability doesn't exceed 80%, but all signals are trending rapidly toward an impending declaration.
